macro_events.event_summary: U.S. Treasury warned global banks of sanctions risk for financing Chinese independent "teapot" refineries that process Iranian crude oil disguised as Malaysian blend, signaling escalation of secondary sanctions enforcement.
primary_drivers: Reduced effective Iranian crude supply to global market via Chinese teapot channel
Higher compliance/financing friction for Iran-Russia barrel buyers, widening Brent-discounted-crude spreads
Bullish marginal-barrel pricing for non-sanctioned crude producers

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated with ~3.8M bpd upstream production; every $1 increase in Brent from tighter Iranian supply flows directly to upstream earnings. No Iranian/Russian crude exposure means pure beneficiary of price lift.
Pure-play beneficiary of Brent uplift with significant Permian and Kazakh production; no sanctioned-barrel exposure. Direct leverage to crude price step-up if Iranian ~1.5M bpd gets squeezed harder.
Pure E&P with highest beta to crude prices among large-caps; no downstream offset. Tighter sanctions enforcement = tighter global supply = direct EPS uplift.
Permian pure-play with high crude-price torque; Diamondback's earnings sensitivity to WTI is among the highest in large-cap E&P, benefits directly from any sanctions-driven price firming.

US Gulf Coast refiner that competes globally with Chinese teapot refiners for product exports (diesel, gasoline) to Latin America/Europe. If teapots lose discounted Iranian feedstock, their cost advantage erodes and Valero's export margins improve.

Largest US refiner by capacity; same teapot-competition dynamic — Chinese teapots running on cheap Iranian barrels have pressured Asian product cracks. Sanctions enforcement could lift global refining margins.
High-beta US oil producer with concentrated Permian exposure and net-debt structure that benefits disproportionately from crude price upside via cash flow deleveraging.

losers.rationale: Petrobras sells heavy Brazilian crude into China; if teapots are starved of Iranian barrels, that's actually mixed — but Petrobras already supplies Chinese state refiners, not teapots, and faces risk if Chinese banks broadly de-risk crude trade financing.

Danaos and other product/crude tanker operators with exposure to the Iran-China dark-fleet trade could see disruption; though tighter sanctions historically lengthen ton-miles (positive), enforcement targeting financing could shrink the trade itself.
Delek and other small US refiners with thin margins face headwind if crude input costs rise faster than product cracks can re-price; smaller refiners have less hedging flexibility than majors.

headlines: U.S. warns banks of sanctions risk over China ‘teapot’ refineries handling Iranian oil

macro_events.event_summary: An unexpected OPEC supply decision (implied production shock) combined with tech sector weakness pressured Wall Street, with Asian markets opening mixed in response.
primary_drivers: OPEC supply shock altering crude oil price trajectory
Cross-asset risk-off rotation into energy and away from high-duration growth

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with direct upstream leverage to crude price moves; an OPEC supply cut lifts realized prices on ~2.3M bpd of production. If the 'shock' is a surprise increase, flip to losers — but headline framing as 'shock' dragging equities suggests bullish-for-oil price action.
Pure peer-correlation play to XOM with similar upstream exposure; moves tightly with WTI/Brent on OPEC headlines.
Permian-concentrated E&P with high oil-price beta (no refining offset); historically among the strongest movers on OPEC supply surprises. Buffett-backed positioning adds flow support.
Oilfield services bellwether — sustained higher crude prices drive E&P capex revisions, directly lifting completions and drilling activity revenue.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs at major US carriers; an OPEC-driven crude spike compresses margins with limited near-term hedging offset and weak pricing power into summer travel.
Most fuel-cost-sensitive major US airline (lower hedge book in recent years, point-to-point domestic model with thinner margins than legacies).
Epicenter of 'tech jitters' framing; highest-beta megacap to AI-trade de-risking and the most-owned crowded long, making it the marginal sell when tech sentiment cracks.
Cruise operators have outsized bunker fuel exposure (~10-12% of opex) and discretionary demand sensitivity; double-hit from higher oil and risk-off tape.

headlines: Asia-Pacific markets open mixed after OPEC shock, tech jitters drag Wall Street lower
